texto: In the West, for example, people believe they must 'pursue happiness' as if it were some kind of a flighty bird that is always out of reach. In the East, we believe we are born with happiness and one of life's important takes, my mother told me, is to protect it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Le Ly Hayslip: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “In the West, for example, people believe they must 'pursue happiness' as if it were some kind of …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, happiness, believe pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de estudos e reflexao.

seo.descriptionSeo: Metáfora atribuída a Le Ly Hayslip: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “In the West, for exampl…

frase_original: In the West, for example, people believe they must 'pursue happiness' as if it were some kind of a flighty bird that is always out of reach. In the East, we believe we are born with happiness and one of life's important takes, my mother told me, is to protect it.

texto: For you see, the face of destiny or luck or god that gives us war also gives us other kinds of pain: the loss of health and youth; the loss of loved ones or of love; the fear that we will end our days alone. Some people suffer in peace the way others suffer in war. The special gift of that suffering, I have learned, is how to be strong while we are weak, how to be brave when we are afraid, how to be wise in the midst of confusion, and how to let go of that which we can no longer hold. In this way, anger can teach us forgiveness, hate can teach us love, and war can teach us peace.
explicacao: Citação de Le Ly Hayslip sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “For you see, the face of destiny or luck or god that gives us war also gives us other kinds of pa…” articula educacao, paz, reflexao em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como termino e luto.
